notes: The engineering layer preserves the UNANIMOUS commentarial restriction: 'asya dharmasya' refers to karma-yoga (niṣkāma-karma) specifically, not to effort generically. Shankara, Ramanuja, Sridhara, Madhva, Keshava all restrict the safety property to this discipline; the engineering layer explicitly names this restriction and discloses the standard misreading ('every little thing helps') as the misreading. The contrast structure (agriculture / medical care / ritual vs karma-yoga) is preserved through Sridhara's three-way contrast. The 'mahato bhayāt' as saṁsāric fear (Ramanuja) is rendered as the engineering form of saṁsāric fear (recurrent unstructured incidents, the 'fear of being inside an incident class for which they have no model') without spiritualising the engineering register. The five commentaries are labeled by tradition (Gaudiya Vaishnava, Advaita, Vishishtadvaita, Rudra Sampradaya, Brahma Sampradaya, Kumara Sampradaya). PASS.
